title: Global Reporting Grants
description: The Pulitzer Center funds international reporting trips on underreported global stories for staff and freelance journalists, photographers, and documentary filmmakers, with no restriction on topic or destination. Grants of roughly $5,000-$10,000 cover the hard costs of a reporting trip, including travel, lodging, and other field expenses, and are awarded on a rolling basis year-round rather than to a fixed cohort or deadline. Applicants must have a firm commitment from a media outlet to publish or air the resulting work. The destination shown here (Kenya) is illustrative only -- in practice the journalist proposes the country as part of their story pitch, and past grants have funded reporting across Africa, Asia, Latin America, and elsewhere.
visaNote: Grant does not include visa-sponsored employment; journalists are responsible for arranging any tourist/journalist visa needed for the reporting destination, though visa costs can typically be included in the grant budget.
